title: antirhea urbaniana
parts: Antirhea urbaniana has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and bark, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of the Antirhea urbaniana plant contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the plant are rich in alkaloids and glycosides, which have been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The bark of the Antirhea urbaniana plant contains tannins and saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat fever and skin conditions.
side_effects: Antirhea urbaniana can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing severe gastrointestinal distress, nausea, and vomiting in individuals with sensitive stomachs. Prolonged consumption can also lead to increased heart rates, palpitations, and in severe cases, cardiac arrhythmias. In some cases, improper use of the plant can cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and respiratory issues. Additionally, excessive consumption of Antirhea urbaniana can lead to kidney damage and impaired liver function, requiring immediate medical attention.
